violations.text: The back storage area floor was observed to have an accumulation of old droppings/debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Per the PIC, debris/droppings to be cleaned same day. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the dry storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the plastic containers used for storage of non-food and non-food contact items were observed to have an accumulation of old droppings. Non-food-contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The back storage area floor was observed to have an accumulation of old droppings/debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

At the time of inspection, a package of shredded cheese was observed to be adulterated by mold growth. Shredded Cheese was voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Sliced Cheese, Shredded Cheese, and Cooked Taco Meat were observed not marked with the date of preparation/thawing. Sliced cheeses and Shredded Cheeses are to be dated with the date of opening, and food prepped on site is to be dated with the date of preparation and held for no longer than 7 days from the date of opening unless frozen. If food item is frozen, the opening of the packaging, or the preparation of the food item is to be counted as day 1, and when the food item is pulled from the freezer for thawing, the item is to be redated/held for use for no longer than 6 days. Cheeses and taco meat were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Popcorn chicken was observed held at 119 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC re-heated food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The floor in the back storage area has an accumulation of debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
